identity.role: Multi-scale geometric hash cache with SpeedLight idempotence

pattern: complete-unformalized work -> formalization in paper-bound space -> Forge

source_work.state_before: MDHG multi-scale geometric cache + self-regulating CA field live in mcp_os/agrm_mdhg_integration/mdhg_ca.py; never formalized, no forge, no proof artifact

adjudicated_divergence: quantize() made total on all reals: the normalized coordinate is clamped into [0,1) before floor, so the bridge is defined on every input (source assumed inputs already in [0,1) and floor of a negative value would underflow before the min-clamp)
eviction made deterministic and replayable via a monotonic admission sequence; the source SlotEntry.last used wall-clock time, making eviction order non-deterministic under equal hit counts
the CA-field self-regulating dynamics (CAField, kernel_step, Wolfram-class assignments, neighborhood relaxation) stay product-side; the forge carries only the proven cache + idempotence core
validated_claims: The bridge dimension is the Leech dimension 24.
Quantize is total on all reals and lands in [0, bins).
Quantize is an idempotent retraction: re-quantizing bin-centered reconstructions is a fixed point.
Slot assignment is deterministic (double SHA-256) and lands on the grid_side x grid_side torus.
SpeedLight idempotence f(f(x)) = f(x): re-admitting the same content is a hit with distance 0 and no admission growth.
Per-slot capacity is never exceeded under heavy load (500 admits, cap 3).
Eviction is deterministic LRU by (hits, seq) and a replay produces the identical final key set.
The admission distance is the minimum Hamming distance over the slot's existing quantized vectors.
The three multi-scale layers (fast/med/slow) are independent.
Occupancy conservation: the grid sum equals the total number of live entries.
honesty_boundary: The CA-field self-regulating dynamics are NOT bound; they need their own stability receipt. Slot collision rate against the double-hash distribution is an empirical product diagnostic, not a paper claim.
tower_link: The 24 dimensions are the Leech dimension; the bridge addresses points of the continuous Leech space constructed exactly in LeechForge (CQE-paper-17). SpeedLight idempotence is the reuse half of the Event Law whose conserved descent is in ChromaForge morphon (CQE-paper-09).
open_obligations: Stability receipt for the CA-field Wolfram-class kernels before promoting the dynamics.

Next profile-repo candidates: CMPLXMCP AGRM controller (auto-escalate inline->MDHG, pairs with this cache), chart8 D4 atlas bijectivity (ConvergeForge obligation), CMPLX-PartsFactory PermForge superpermutation.
notes: Ninth promotion of the loop; fourth from the profile-repo lane. Completes a tight triangle in the corpus: LeechForge builds the 24D space (paper 17), MDHGForge addresses it as a discrete-continuous bridge with idempotent reuse (paper 07), and ChromaForge morphon supplies the conserved descent the Event Law mints on each admission (paper 09).
